text:
1.
                                                                                                                Introduction: This document sets out how a requestor should construct a jwt-auth header and how a receiver must validate the header.

2.
                                                                                                                Requirements
                                                                                                                for the
                                                                                                                LFI as
                                                                                                                the
                                                                                                                Client
                                                                                                                (sending
                                                                                                                a token): These examples are specific to the requests from the LFI to the API Hub CM and HH endpoints.

metadata.section:
2.
                                                                                                                Requirements
                                                                                                                for the
                                                                                                                LFI as
                                                                                                                the
                                                                                                                Client
                                                                                                                (sending
                                                                                                                a token)

text:
2.1
                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing
                                                                                                                keys on
                                                                                                                a JWKS: The LFI must create an Application in their OFTF Organisation labelled C3-hh-cm-client.

metadata.section:
2.1
                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing
                                                                                                                keys on
                                                                                                                a JWKS

text:
2.1
                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing
                                                                                                                keys on
                                                                                                                a JWKS: The LFI must create both the C3 transport client and Sig4 transport signing certificates in this Application using code snippets provided in the OFTF.

metadata.section:
2.1
                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing
                                                                                                                keys on
                                                                                                                a JWKS

text:
2.1
                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing
                                                                                                                keys on
                                                                                                                a JWKS: The OFTF will create a unique KID and host the JWKS.

metadata.section:
2.1
                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing
                                                                                                                keys on
                                                                                                                a JWKS

text:
2.1
                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing
                                                                                                                keys on
                                                                                                                a JWKS: When publishing a new key, the LFI must wait for 10 minutes before issuing a message with the key (to allow the receiver’s caches to refresh).

                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing

                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of

                                                                                                                of

                                                                                                                a JWKS

text: 2.2 Creating the authorisation header: The LFI must ensure that the machine on which the signature is generated uses NTP to synchronise its clock.
2.2 Creating the authorisation header: The LFI must construct the header and payload for the JWT as specified in JWT Authorization | 3. JWT Auth Claims Reference .
2.2 Creating the authorisation header reference: JWT Authorization | 3. JWT Auth Claims Reference
2.2 Creating the authorisation header: The JWT must be signed using the PS256 algorithm using a private signing key in the C3-hh-cm-client application.
2.2 Creating the authorisation header: The JWT must be included as a bearer token in the authorization http header.

2.2 Creating the authorisation header: The https request must be made over mutual tls. The C3 client certificate must be used to initiate the mutual tls session, and must have a DN and OU that matches the values placed in the signature.
3. Requirements for the LFI as the Server (receiving a token): These examples are specific to the requests from the API Hub to the LFI Ozone Connect API.

metadata.section: 3. Requirements for the LFI as the Server (receiving a token)

text: 3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: The receiver must ensure that the machine on which the signature is verified uses NTP to synchronise its clock.
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: The receiver must ensure that the request was received over a mutual tls connection.
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: The receiver must extract the jwt-auth token from the authorization http header.
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: The JWT must verify the signature on the JWT using the kid specified in the JWS
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: The JWKS URI is derived from the requestor client certificate subject. The OU and CN values are substituted as follows:
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: Example requestor client certificate subject CN=ABC, OU=XYZ, O=Organisation Name, C=AE
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: Template for Sandbox JWKS https://keystore.sandbox.directory.openfinance.ae/${OU}/${CN}/application.jwks
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: Example JWKS URI: https://keystore.sandbox.directory.openfinance.ae/XYZ/ABC/application.jwks
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: The receiver may cache the JWKS for up to ten minutes.
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header: The receiver must verify each of the claims in the JWT has the expected value specified in JWT Authorization | 3. JWT Auth Claims Reference
3.1 Verifying the authorisation header reference: JWT Authorization | 3. JWT Auth Claims Reference
4.1 Header table: Claim Name | Expected Value | Mandatory | Notes
4.1 Header table: alg | PS256 | Yes |
4.1 Header table: typ | JOSE | Yes |
4.1 Header table: cty | json | Yes |

4.1 Header table: kid | The key ID of key-pair used to sign the message as published on the JWKS. | Yes | The specification does not support the use of other means of identifying the key as they are not considered to be secure enough (e.g. x5c and x5u are not supported).
4.1 Header: Claim Name
4.1 Header: Expected Value
4.1 Header: Mandatory
4.1 Header: Notes
4.1 Header: alg
4.1 Header: PS256
4.1 Header: Yes
4.1 Header:
4.1 Header: typ
4.1 Header: JOSE
4.1 Header: Yes
4.1 Header:

4.1 Header: json
4.1 Header: Yes
4.1 Header:
4.1 Header: kid
4.1 Header: The key ID of key-pair used to sign the message as published on the JWKS.
4.1 Header: Yes
4.1 Header: The specification does not support the use of other means of identifying the key as they are not considered to be secure enough (e.g. x5c and x5u are not supported).
4.2 Body table: Claim Name | Expected Value | Mandatory | Example value for JWT issued by Ozone | Notes

4.2 Body table: iss | The organization O in the TLS certificate Subject that was used in the transport layer. | Yes | Ozone Financial Technology Limited | For a certificate with Subject ofCN=ABC, OU=XYZ, O=RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED, C=UK,this would be set to RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED

4.2 Body table: sub | The organization unit OU in the TLS certificate Subject that was used in the transport layer. | Yes |  | For a certificate with Subject of CN=ABC, OU=XYZ, O=RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED, C=UK, this would be set to XYZ
4.2 Body table: aud | Identifier for the party receiving the JWT. | Yes |  | This must be set to the PROVIDER_ID specified by Ozone during configuration.

4.2 Body table: exp | Time when the JWT will expire in UTC seconds since epoch. | Yes | 30 | We recommend an expiry time of 10-30s.When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is greater than this value.

4.2 Body table: iat | Time when the JWT was issued in UTC seconds since epoch. | Yes |  | When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is less than this value.

4.2 Body table: nbf | Time before which the JWT is invalid. | No |  | When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is less than this value (when specified).
4.2 Body table: jti | A unique identifier for the JWT. | Yes |  | We recommend that the requestor populates this value with a uuidv4. This increases the entropy for the JWT.
